---
title: "Get started"
---

## Install

The core package is on CRAN:

```r
install.packages("aisdk")
```

Companion packages are installed only when you need their capabilities. Once
they are on CRAN you will be able to:

```r
install.packages("aisdk.providers")     # extra provider adapters
install.packages("aisdk.orchestration")  # multi-agent Flow / Team / Mission
install.packages("aisdk.mcp")            # Model Context Protocol
install.packages("aisdk.skills")         # skill ecosystem
install.packages("aisdk.channels")       # messaging integrations
install.packages("aisdk.datatools")      # charts & reports
install.packages("aisdk.shiny")          # Shiny web UI
install.packages("aisdk.slm")            # local model inference
```

Development versions live under [github.com/YuLab-SMU](https://github.com/YuLab-SMU):

```r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("YuLab-SMU/aisdk")
pak::pak("YuLab-SMU/aisdk.providers")
```

## Configure a provider

Most providers read their API key from an environment variable. For the core
OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini providers:

```r
Sys.setenv(OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-...")
```

## A first conversation

```r
library(aisdk)

chat <- create_chat_session("openai:gpt-4o")
generate_text(chat, "Give me three ideas for a talk on R and LLMs.")
```

## Add providers without changing your code

Companion packages register themselves with the core registry on load, so the
`provider:model` syntax just works once the package is installed:

```r
library(aisdk.providers)   # registers deepseek, kimi, xai, ... on load
generate_text(create_chat_session("deepseek:deepseek-chat"), "Hello!")
```

If you ask for a provider that lives in a companion package you have not
installed yet, the core will tell you exactly which package to install (and, in
an interactive session, offer to install it for you).

## Where to next

- Browse each package from the **Packages** menu above.
- Start with the [**aisdk** core](packages/aisdk.qmd) for the unified
  interface, agents, tools, and skills.
